title: 第 159 節
en: ' For her brightness and gloiy, I will offer her a sacrifice ....
62. ' Offer up a sacrifice, O Spitama Zarathurtra ! unto this ^ring of mine, Ardvi Sftra Anihita ....

53. 'To her did the valiant warrior Tusa^* offer » Doubtful (Bundahi^ XXII, 2). It is the same as Lake Urumiah. Its name b miswritten in Firdausi (Khan^ast for Al&^ast, ^r- 7^^ for ' Doubtful; see Etudes Iraniennes,ir,uruy&pa» P- 179* ■ The White Forest (iWd.). ' Aurvasira (ibid).
■ Doubtful. • Trying to flee and escape. ^* Possibly, ' vieing in horses ' (for the swiftness of the race) :
cf. Yt XIX, 77.
^ Tusa, in the Sh&h Ndmah Tus; one of the most celebrated Pahlavans of Kai Khosrav; he was the son of king Naotara (Nddar).

AbAn ya^t. 67 worship on the back of his horse ^, begging swiftness for his teams, health for his own body, and that he might watch with full success* those who hated him. smite down his foes, and destroy at one stroke his adversaries, his enemies, and those who hated him \ 54. 'He begged of her a boon, saying: "Grant me this, O good, most beneficent Ardvi Sflra An&hita 1 that I may overcome the gallant sons of Va6saka *, by the castle Khshathr6-saoka, that stands high up on the lofty, holy Kangha* ; that I may smite of the Turanian people their fifties and their hundreds, theu" hundreds and their thousands, their thousands and their tens of thousands, their tens of thousands and their myriads of myriads/'
55. * Ardvi Sdra An&hita granted him that boon •, as he was offering libations, giving gifts, sacrificing, and entreating that she would grant him that boon.
^ He offers not a fuU. sacrifice, being on horseback.

member was Plr&n Vtsah, the ckver and upright minister of Afrisy&b, the Turanian Nestor; but his counsels were despised for the common ruin, and himself perished with aU his sons in the war against Ir&n.

exile, in a part of the land of Khv&rizm, which is described as an earthly paradise. TbLs city was built on the top of a high mountain (Antare-Kangha, Yt. XIX, 4). The Khshathrd-saoka castle is called in the Sh&h N&mah Kang des, *the fortress of Kangha/ and, possibly, Khshathr6-saoka is a mere epithet of dvarem, ' the castle of kingly welfare.'
Kai Khosrav himself.
F 2 68 YA8TS AND stR6ZAHS.

56. 'Olla'iq)aiacri6ceyOSpita]iiaZaiadiiiftialimtotliiss|^^ of wiotf Ardvi SAni AnUuta • • • • 57* 'To her did the gallant sons of Va^saka offer tip a sacrifice in the castle Khshathrd-saoka, that stands high up on the lofty, holy Kangha, with a hundred male hordes, a thousand oxen, ten thousand lambs.

58* 'They begged of her a boon, saying: "Grant us this, O good, most beneficent Ardvi Stm Andhita! that we may overcome the valiant warrior Tusa, and that we may smite of the Aryan people their fifties and their hundreds, their hundreds and their thousands, their thousands sbid their tens of thousands, thdr tens of thousands and their myriads of myriads \'* 59* 'Ardvi Sftra Andhita did not grant them that boon« 'For ber brightness and glorjr, I will offer her a XVL 6o« * Offer up a sacrifice, O Spttama ZanUbiutni I unto this spring of mine, Ardvi Sftra Anflhita . . . • 6i. 'The old' Vafra Nav4za worshipped her, when the strong fiend-smiter, Thra^taona, flung him up in the air in the shape of a bird, of a vulture '.

' Doabtfiil (poiirY6) ; perhaps ' the man of the primitive faith ' (the paoirjd'/katsha ; cf: Yt XIII, o, note) : the sacrifice he offers is quite a Zoroastrian one (cf. §$17, 104, and note a to the latter).

Thratouma cjclns, of which no other trace is found in the Avesta (except in Yt XXIII, 4). It referred most likelj to the time when AbAn vast. 69 62. * He went on flying, for three days and three nights, towards his own house; but he could not, he could not turn down. At the end of the third night, when the beneficent dawn came dawning up, then he prayed unto Ardvi Sftra An&hita, saying :

63. ' "Ardvi SAra AnAhital do thou quickly hasten helpfully and bring me assistance at once. I will offer thee a thousand libatibns, cleanly prepared and well strained, along with Haomas and meat, by the brink of the river Rangha, if I reach alive the earth made by Ahura and my own house."

64. 'Ardvi Sdra An^ita hastened unto him in the shape of a maid, fair of body, most strong, tallformed^ high-girded, pure, nobly bom of a glorious race, wearing shoes up to the ankle, wearing a golden . . • • \ and radiant \ 65. ' She seized him by the arm : quickly was it done, nor was it long till, speeding, he arrived at the earth made by Mazda and at his own house, safe, unhurt, unwounded, just as he was before.

[66. 'Ardvi SAra An&hita granted him that boon, as he was offering up libations, giving gifts, sacrificing, entreating that she would grant him that boon^.] < For her brightness and glory, I will offer her a sacrifice ....

Thrattaona, on his march to Bawri, the capital of A«i (cf. f 39), arrived at the Tigris (the Rangha) ; an angel then came and taught bim magic to enable him to baffle the sortileges of Asi (Sh&h N&mah). We have in this passage an mstance of his talents as a wizard, and one which helps us to understand why Thra£taona is considered as the inventor of magic, and his name is invoked in spells and incantations (Hamzah Ispahanensis, p. loi ; Anquetili Ilf pp* 136 seq.). Cf. Yt XIV, 40 and note.
^ Urvikhxna, a word of doubtful meaning.
• Cf.YtV, 78, ia6.
' This dause is no doubt spurious here* 70 YA^S AND shldZAHS.
67. * Offer up a sacrifice^ O Spitama Zarathiutra 1 unto this spring of mine, Ardvi Sftra Anihita ....

68. * To her did G^imAspa^ offer up a sacrifice, with a hundred horses, a thousand oxen, ten thousand lambs, when he saw the ^army of the wicked, of the worshippers of the Dafivas, coming from afar in battle array.
69. * He asked of her a boon, saying : '* Grant me this, O good, most beneficent Ardvi S6ra Anihita ! that I may be as constantly victorious as any one of all the Aryans ^"
70. * Ardvi SOra Anihita granted him that boon, as he was offering up libations, giving gifts, sacrificing, ^nd entreating that she would grant him that boon.
' For her brightness and glory, I will offer her a sacrifice ....
f 1. < Offer up a sacrifice, O Spitama Zarathartra I unto this spring of mine, Ardvi Sfira Andbita ....
zh: 為了她的光輝和榮耀，我將向她獻上祭品……
62. 「斯皮塔瑪‧瑣羅亞斯德啊，向我這道光環獻祭吧，向阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿尼希塔獻祭……

53. 「英勇的戰士圖薩（Tusa）向她獻祭。」*「可疑者」（Bundahi^ XXII, 2）。這與烏魯米亞湖（Lake Urumiah）相同。其名稱在菲爾道西（Firdausi）中被誤寫（Khan^ast 作 Al&^ast，^r- 7^^ 作「可疑者」；參閱《伊朗研究》（Etudes Iraniennes）, ii, p. 179）。白色森林（iWd.）。奧瓦西拉（Aurvasira）（ibid）。
■ 有疑問。• 試圖逃離和躲避。^* 可能為「在馬匹上競爭」（為了比賽的速度）：
參閱《雅斯納經》第十九篇，第七十七節（cf. Yt XIX, 77）。
^ 圖薩（Tusa），見《沙王紀》（Shāh Nāmah）中的圖薩；凱‧霍斯罗夫時代最著名的帕赫拉萬（Pahlavan）之一；他是國王瑙塔拉（Naotara，即那達爾〔Nādar〕）之子。

在馬背上敬拜，祈求他的隊伍迅捷，祈求他自身的身體健康，並祈求他能以完全的成功監視那些仇恨他的人。擊倒他的敵人，一舉消滅他的對手、他的敵人，以及那些仇恨他的人。 54. 他向她祈求恩賜，說：「求妳賜予我此願，善良的、最仁慈的阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿那希塔（Ardvi Sûra Anahita）啊！願我能克勝瓦茲薩卡（Vâsaka）的英勇兒子們，在那屹立於高聳聖山康哈（Kangha）之上的克薩特洛‧薩歐卡堡（Khshathra-saoka）之處；願我能擊殺圖蘭人民的五十人、百人、百人與千人、千人與萬人、萬人與千萬之眾！」
55. * 當他奉獻灌禮、獻上禮物、進行祭祀，並懇求她賜予那項恩惠時，阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿納希塔（Ardvi Sdra Anhita）便將那項恩惠賜予了他。
他騎馬時未獻全祭。
普爾&n 瓦茨&h 是非力西亞(Afrisy&b)的聰慧而正直的大臣，圖蘭人的涅斯托爾(Turanian Nestor)，但他的勸告遭到漠視，終致共同毀滅，他本人與其所有兒子都在對伊朗(Ir&n)的戰爭中喪生。

流放地在花剌子模地區（Khvārizm）的某處，該地被描述為一片人間樂園。該城市建立在一座高山山頂上（Antare-Kangha, Yt. XIX, 4）。Khshathṛa-saoka 城堡在《沙赫納默》（Shāh Nāmah）中被稱為康格德斯（Kangdes），即「康格堡壘」（the fortress of Kangha），而且 Khshathṛa-saoka 可能只是 dvarem（「王威之城堡」）的一個修飾語。
凱‧霍斯瑞夫本人。
F 2 68 雅什特與西羅扎。
56. 「……斯皮塔瑪‧查拉圖斯特拉啊，向我這泉水、阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿納希塔獻祭……」57. 瓦薩卡（Va^saka）的勇敢兒子們在高聳於崇高聖山康哈（Kangha）上的克沙特拉‧紹卡城堡（Khshathrd-saoka）中向她獻祭，獻上一百隊男性軍旅、一千頭牛、一萬隻羔羊。

58* 「他們向她祈求一個恩賜，說道：『賜予我們此恩，妳至善、至仁慈的阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧安德希塔（Ardvi Sûra Andhita）啊！願我們能夠戰勝英勇的戰士圖薩（Tusa），願我們能夠擊殺阿利安人民的五十人和百人，百人和千人，千人和萬人，萬人和無數的無數啊。』」 59* 「阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧安德希塔並未賜予他們此恩。『為了她的光輝與榮耀，我將向她獻上祭祀。』」 60* 「斯皮塔瑪‧查拉圖斯特拉啊，向我的此泉水獻祭吧，向阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧安德希塔獻祭……」 61* 「古老的瓦傑拉‧那瓦札（Vafra Navâza）曾經崇拜她，當那強大的惡魔克星、撒頓（Thraêtaona），將他拋擲於空中，化作鳥類、禿鷹的形狀之時。」
「可疑的（詩文）；或許『原始信仰之人』（paoirjd'/katsha；參見《雅施特經》第十三卷第6節註）：他所獻祭完全是祆教式的（參見§17、104及後者的註a）。
Thratouma cjclns 在《阿維斯陀》中別無他處可考（除《雅什特》第二十三篇第四節外）。它最可能指涉《阿邦雅什特》的時代。69 62. * 他繼續飛行三日三夜，朝自己的家而去；但他不能、不能轉身下降。第三夜終了，當仁慈的黎明升起時，他便向阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿納希塔祈禱，說道：
63. 「『願妳迅速前來，阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿納希塔(Ardvi Sāra Anāhita)，以援助之心相助於我。倘若我能活著抵達阿胡拉(Ahura)所造之地與我自己的家宅，我將於蘭格哈河(Rangha)之濱，獻祭予妳千次潔淨調製、精心過濾之奠酒，並奉上獻香草(Haoma)與肉祭。』」
64. 阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿尼塔（Ardvi Sdra Anâita）以少女的姿態急速前往迎接他，身形優美、力量無窮、身材高挑、腰身挺直、純潔無瑕、出身於光榮的高貴種族，穿著及踝之履，穿戴著金色……並光芒四射。 65. 她抓住他的手臂；動作迅捷，不久之後，他疾馳而至，抵達了馬茲達（Mazda）所造的大地與他自己的家宅，安全無恙、未曾受傷、未曾受損，宛如他之前的模樣。
[66. 'Ardvi Sura Anahita 在他奉獻灌祭、贈予禮物、進行祭祀，並懇求她賜予該恩典之際，便賜予了他該恩典^。] < 為了她的光輝與榮耀，我將向她獻上祭祀……

薩迦那(Thrattaona)在進軍往艾希(Açi)的首都巴烏里(Bawri)的途中，抵達底格里斯河(蘭格河，Rangha)；此時一位天使前來教導他魔法，使他能夠挫敗艾希王的巫術(《沙赫那瑪》，Shāh Nāmah)。在此段記載中，我們看到薩迦那作為巫師的才能之一例，這也有助於我們理解為何薩迦那被視為魔法的發明者，且其名字在咒語和呪文中被援引(哈姆札‧伊斯法罕尼《著作》，第101頁；安凱蒂爾‧迪佩魯《著作》，第136頁及以後各頁)。參閱《耶斯納頌》第十四卷第40節及其注釋。
^ Urvikhxna，一詞意義不明。
• 參見《薄伽梵歌》(Yajnavalkya Smriti)，78‧1a6。
此條款無疑在此處是偽造的*70 雅斯與舍德札。
67. * 獻祭吧，俄斯帕瑪‧查拉圖斯特拉啊^，獻祭給我這泉水，阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿尼希塔……
68. * 當吉瑪斯帕（Gimāspa）看見惡人之軍隊，即那些魔鬼（Daivas）的崇拜者，從遠方以陣列之姿態前來時，他向她獻祭，獻上一百匹馬、一千頭牛、一萬隻羔羊。
69. * 他向她祈求一個恩惠，說：「請賜予我此事，哦良善而最慈悲的阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿尼希塔（Ardvi Sôra Anihita）！願我如同所有雅利安人中任何一位一樣地常保勝利。」 *
70. * 阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿尼希塔女神（Ardvi Sōra Anihita）在他奉獻灑奠、獻禮、祭祀並懇求她賜予此福祐之際，遂准許了他所求之福祐。
為了她的光輝與榮耀，我將向她獻祭……
f 1. < 奉獻一項祭祀，啊斯皮塔瑪‧查拉圖斯特拉（Spitama Zarathustra）！獻給我這道泉源，阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿那西塔（Ardvi Sûra Anahita）....
